Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): enable mono-cache inline path for closure calls

Previously, closure calls (ctx_ptr != 0) were forced to a slow path
that called jit_runtime_get_jit_entry + jit_runtime_finish_direct_call
on EVERY iteration (2 FFI crossings per call). This was because the
cached ctx_ptr was considered potentially dangling.

However, the ctx_ptr is safe for monomorphic cache hits because:
- callee_i64 match guarantees the same closure (same heap handle)
- The closure's Rc<BytecodeArray> keeps the context Rc alive
- The callee receives context via RSI (direct context slot stubs),
  not from the global TLS context cell

Now closures use the same zero-FFI mono-cache inline path as plain
functions: BA save/restore inline + direct CALL to entry point.
This eliminates ~200-300 cycles of per-call overhead.

Expected impact: closure_counter benchmark should improve significantly
(was SIGSEGV or 316x behind V8, now using the same fast path as
non-closure calls).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: change API ingress to INGRESS_TRAFFIC_ALL and add workflow_dispatch to deploy

Cloud Run-to-Cloud Run calls in the same project are NOT automatically
treated as internal traffic unless VPC egress is configured. Without a
Serverless VPC Access Connector or Direct VPC Egress on the UI service,
requests from the nginx BFF are seen as external by the API ingress and
rejected with 404.

Security is maintained by Cloud Run IAM: the API requires a valid OIDC
token from osrs-ui-sa (roles/run.invoker). Unauthenticated public
requests receive 403.

Also adds workflow_dispatch to the deploy workflow so the ingress change
can be applied without triggering a full image rebuild.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove absolute /tmp/ paths from e2e artifact upload

When upload-artifact@v4 includes absolute paths (/tmp/...), all files
in the artifact are stored using their full absolute paths. This caused
e2e-summary.json to be stored as
  home/runner/work/chater/chater/e2e-summary.json
instead of at the artifact root, breaking the post-summary job.

Remove /tmp/ paths from the artifact upload to keep all files at root.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(bench): unified 3-phase Maglev warmup for all precompiled benchmarks

Refactored all 9 precompiled benchmarks to use warmup_with_maglev() helper:
- Phase 1: 100 interpreter iterations to warm ICs + trigger Maglev
- Phase 2: Reset deopt counter + 100 JIT validation iterations (warms JIT ICs)
- Phase 3: Final deopt reset before Criterion measurement

Also capped deopt backoff at 2^10 (1024) instead of 2^20 (1M) to allow
recovery from transient deopts like cold JIT ICs after compilation.

This ensures Maglev is properly warmed AND its inline caches populated
before benchmarking, giving representative JIT performance numbers.

commit.message:
fix(ci): fix YAML literal block heredoc indentation in post-ci workflow

Python heredoc content was at column 0, causing GitHub's YAML parser
to end the run: | literal block early and fail to parse the workflow
(all previous 9 runs showed 'failure' with 0 jobs for this reason).

Fix: indent all Python heredoc content and PY terminators with 10
spaces, matching the pattern used in ci.yml. YAML strips the block
indentation when extracting the value, so bash receives the Python
code at column 0 (correct for heredoc) and 'PY' at column 0 (correct
for heredoc termination).

dependencies. These packages are now installed on-demand:
